user_prompt: How many unique customers are there in the state of Sao Paulo?
accepted_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_unique_id) AS unique_customers_sp FROM customers WHERE customer_state = 'SP';

user_prompt: What is the average shipping freight value charged for order items?
accepted_sql: SELECT AVG(freight_value) AS avg_freight FROM order_items;

user_prompt: List all order statuses and how many orders are currently in each status.
column5: COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders GROUP BY order_status ORDER BY order_count DESC;

user_prompt: Show our top 5 cities based on total payment value received.
column5: SUM(p.payment_value) AS total_payments FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN order_payments p ON o.order_id = p.order_id GROUP BY c.customer_city ORDER BY total_payments DESC LIMIT 5;

user_prompt: Calculate the average review score specifically for product items translated to the english category 'health_beauty'.
accepted_sql: SELECT AVG(r.review_score) AS avg_review_score FROM order_reviews r JOIN orders o ON r.order_id = o.order_id JOIN order_items i ON o.order_id = i.order_id JOIN products p ON i.product_id = p.product_id JOIN product_category_name_translation t ON p.product_category_name = t.product_category_name WHERE t.product_category_name_english = 'health_beauty';

user_prompt: Identify the top 3 sellers who generated the most gross revenue in 2017.
column5: SUM(i.price) AS gross_revenue FROM sellers s JOIN order_items i ON s.seller_id = i.seller_id JOIN orders o ON i.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.order_purchase_timestamp >= '2017-01-01 00:00:00' AND o.order_purchase_timestamp <= '2017-12-31 23:59:59' GROUP BY s.seller_id ORDER BY gross_revenue DESC LIMIT 3;

user_prompt: What percentage of orders are paid using credit card compared to other payment types?
column6: (COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) FROM order_payments)) AS percentage FROM order_payments GROUP BY payment_type ORDER BY percentage DESC;

user_prompt: Show the month-over-month growth rate in total order count for the first half of 2018.
column6: COUNT(order_id) AS current_month_orders FROM orders WHERE order_purchase_timestamp >= '2018-01-01 00:00:00' AND order_purchase_timestamp <= '2018-06-30 23:59:59' GROUP BY 1)
column9: LAG(current_month_orders) OVER (ORDER BY order_month) AS prev_month_orders FROM monthly_orders) SELECT order_month

user_prompt: Calculate the average delivery time delta in days (difference between purchase and actual customer delivery date) for completed orders group by destination customer state.
column5: AVG(EPOCH(order_delivered_customer_date - order_purchase_timestamp) / 86400.0) AS avg_delivery_days FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered' AND o.order_delivered_customer_date IS NOT NULL AND o.order_purchase_timestamp IS NOT NULL GROUP BY c.customer_state ORDER BY avg_delivery_days DESC;

user_prompt: Find customers who have placed at least 5 distinct orders on separate purchase days.
column6: o.order_purchase_timestamp)) AS distinct_purchase_days FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_unique_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT DATE_TRUNC('day'

user_prompt: Calculate the total Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) for all successfully delivered orders.
accepted_sql: SELECT SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value) AS gmv FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered';

user_prompt: Determine our overall logistics performance by calculating the on-time delivery rate as a percentage of all delivered orders.
accepted_sql: SELECT (COUNT(CASE WHEN order_delivered_customer_date <= order_estimated_delivery_date THEN 1 END) * 100.0) / COUNT() AS on_time_delivery_rate FROM orders WHERE order_status = 'delivered';

user_prompt: What is the Average Order Value (AOV) for delivered orders placed by customers in the state of Rio de Janeiro (RJ)?
accepted_sql: SELECT SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value) / COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id) AS aov_rj FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered' AND c.customer_state = 'RJ';

user_prompt: Find the total late deliveries and their average customer review score grouped by seller state showing only states with more than 50 late deliveries.
column5: COUNT(CASE WHEN o.order_delivered_customer_date > o.order_estimated_delivery_date THEN 1 END) AS late_deliveries
column6: AVG(r.review_score) AS avg_late_review_score FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id JOIN sellers s ON oi.seller_id = s.seller_id JOIN order_reviews r ON o.order_id = r.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered' GROUP BY s.seller_state HAVING COUNT(CASE WHEN o.order_delivered_customer_date > o.order_estimated_delivery_date THEN 1 END) > 50 ORDER BY late_deliveries DESC;

user_prompt: Analyze customer retention by calculating the total GMV contribution and overall review rate of repeat customers vs single-purchase customers.
column7: COUNT(DISTINCT r.review_id) * 1.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS customer_review_rate FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id LEFT JOIN order_reviews r ON o.order_id = r.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered' GROUP BY c.customer_unique_id)
column9: CASE WHEN delivered_orders > 1 THEN 'Repeat Customer' ELSE 'Single Purchase Customer' END AS segment_type
column14: AVG(customer_review_rate) AS avg_segment_review_rate FROM customer_segments GROUP BY segment_type;
